_meta.title: The Six Sign Polarities (reflex pairs + co-governed systems)
_meta.lineage: Medical-astrology tradition — the six axes of opposite signs and the body systems they jointly govern (Hill's reflex principle: an afflicted zone mirrors to its opposite). Distilled, attributed to the tradition.
_meta.note: The opposite (180°) and two squares (90°) of an afflicted sign give its REFLEX zones. These pairs name the polarity + the system the two signs co-regulate.

polarities.co_system: the cranial–renal / adrenal axis (head pressure mirrors to the kidneys and lower back)
the throat–pelvic axis (the upper and lower gates of the body)
the upper-limb / lung–hip / thigh axis (the body's reach and stride; nerves and sciatic)
the stomach–skeletal axis (soft holding mirrors to bone and joint; the knees reflex to the chest/stomach)
the heart–circulation axis (the central pump and the peripheral flow)
the gut–lymph axis (assimilation and elimination of fluids; digestion mirrors to the feet/lymph)

co_rulers._note: Modern co-rulers, surfaced for completeness; the traditional ruler is primary for the WHERE→default-WHAT mapping.
